Protecting our green spaces from inappropriate housing developments and safeguarding our precious environment.

  • Tweet

This has been a pledge since I was first elected as MP in 2010, starting with the Save Fant Farm campaign. We now face challenges with the huge Bradbourne and Broadwater Farm developments in the Mallings, and the threat to Bunyards Farm in Allington

News

Helen Grant MP Writes to MBC Planning Committee to Oppose Bunyards Planning Application

Thursday, 23 April, 2026
Ahead of tonight's MBC planning committee, I have sent the following statement to the committee members, outlining my strong opposition to the Bunyards planning application:

Helen Grant MP Details Problems with Over-Development in the County

Wednesday, 8 April, 2026
Throughout my time as a Member of Parliament here in the Garden of England, overdevelopment has been one of the most persistent concerns raised by constituents.I have consistently worked to protect our precious green spaces from inappropriate development, from fighting to save Fant Farm in Maidstone

Helen Grant MP writes to Tonbridge and Malling Borough Council to oppose 1,300-home Bradbourne development in Aylesford

Thursday, 5 March, 2026
Helen Grant MP has written to Tonbridge and Malling Borough Councillors urging them to reject an application to build a new community of 1,300 homes to be known as Bradbourne on land east of Kiln Barn Road and west of Hermitage Lane in Aylesford.

Helen Grant Condemns No-Show of South East Water CEO David Hinton at Tunbridge Wells Borough Council Scrutiny Committee

Friday, 23 January, 2026
Amidst the turmoil and failure caused by South East Water, South East Water CEO David Hinton shamefully refused to attend the Tunbridge Wells Borough Council Scrutiny Committee, on the very same day that invitations were sent by SEW invited MPs to a webinar to discuss bill increases.

Maidstone and Malling MP Helen Grant complains of too many road closures - and residents agree

Saturday, 17 January, 2026
Motorists must be able to go about their daily lives without sitting in unnecessary and prolonged traffic. Please read more here:

Calls for water boss to quit amid latest supply crisis

Tuesday, 13 January, 2026
Once again, South East Water has failed, and once again there has been a shocking lack of leadership.This is no isolated incident, it is now a familiar and unacceptable pattern across Kent and Sussex.It’s been like the nightmare before Christmas we experienced for 12 days in 2022.

Helen Grant MP leads ‘time sensitive’ charge against 1300 houses between Hermitage Lane and Ditton

Friday, 19 April, 2024
April 2024, Maidstone/Malling, Kent: The fight against overdevelopment in the Hermitage Lane area stepped up a gear after a packed public meeting and official opposition from Helen Grant MP last week.
